turns.content:
The advisor document has been written successfully to `.planning/research/hard-restructuring-advisor-2026-05-21.md` (151 lines). 

Here's a summary of what it covers:

**5 Gray-Area Decisions Analyzed:**

| Decision | Options Analyzed | Recommended |
|----------|-----------------|-------------|
| **Phase Ordering** | Non-destructive first vs. Architecture first vs. Interleaved | **Architecture first** — plugin.ts at 493/500 LOC is actively constraining; delegation dual-facade is highest-complexity debt |
| **Legacy Removal** | Remove all vs. Per-file gates vs. Defer | **Per-file gates** — aligns with "Iteratively Granular" pillar; typed errors as one-wave exception |
| **Dependency Cleanup** | Remove before vs. Remove after vs. Bump only | **Remove after restructuring** with a baseline scan first; committed as dedicated phase slot |
| **Plugin.ts Decomposition** | Minimal (tool registry) vs. Full vs. Rewrite | **Full decomposition** — extract startup + tool-registry + hook-composition into 3 phases; plugin.ts → ~150 LOC |
| **Soft Routing** | Full runtime vs. Soft-only vs. Hybrid registry | **Hybrid** — read-side lineage-resolver.ts (~50 LOC) for doctor validation; skills stay soft |

**Recommended Phase Sequence:** Phase 18.1 through 18.7, ordered by dependency: plugin decomposition first (unblocks everything), then type safety, sync I/O, errors, lineage resolver, then dependency cleanup — all before Phase 19.
